title: New Year's Eve, 1913
content:
O, Cartmel bells ring soft to-night,
    And Cartmel bells ring clear,
    But I lie far away to-night,
    Listening with my dear;

title: Babel: The Gate Of The God
content:
Lost towers impend, copeless primeval props
    Of the new threatening sky, and first rude digits
    Of awe remonstrance and uneasy power

title: Midsummer Eve
content:
Midsummer Eve




        TO CLINTON BALMER
        AND THE DEAR MEMORY OF
        JAMES HAMILTON HAY
        FOR THE SUMMER OF 1900

title: King Lear's Wife
content:
TO T. STURGE MOORE


     The years come on, the years go by,
     And in my Northern valley I,
     Withdrawn from life, watch life go by.
